sn35.97:1.1: “Monks, I will teach you about one who dwells in heedlessness and one who dwells in heedfulness.
sn35.97:1.2: Listen & pay careful attention, I will speak.”
sn35.97:1.3: “And how does one dwell in heedlessness?
sn35.97:1.4: When a monk dwells without restraint over the faculty of the eye, the mind is stained with forms cognizable via the eye.
sn35.97:1.5: When the mind is stained, there is no joy.
sn35.97:1.6: There being no joy, there is no rapture.
sn35.97:1.7: There being no rapture, there is no calm.
sn35.97:1.8: There being no calm, he dwells in pain.
sn35.97:1.9: When pained, the mind does not become centered.
sn35.97:1.10: When the mind is uncentered, phenomena don’t become manifest.
sn35.97:1.11: When phenomena aren’t manifest, he is reckoned simply as one who dwells in heedlessness...
sn35.97:1.12: “When a monk dwells without restraint over the tongue…
sn35.97:1.15: “When a monk dwells without restraint over the faculty of the intellect, the mind is stained with ideas cognizable via the intellect.
sn35.97:1.16: When the mind is stained, there is no joy.
sn35.97:1.17: There being no joy, there is no rapture.
sn35.97:1.18: There being no rapture, there is no calm.
sn35.97:1.19: There being no calm, he dwells in pain.
sn35.97:1.20: When pained, the mind does not become centered.
sn35.97:1.21: When the mind is uncentered, phenomena don’t become manifest.
sn35.97:1.22: When phenomena aren’t manifest, he is reckoned simply as one who dwells in heedlessness.
sn35.97:1.23: “This is how one dwells in heedlessness.
sn35.97:2.1: “And how does one dwell in heedfulness?
sn35.97:2.2: When a monk dwells with restraint over the faculty of the eye, the mind is not stained with forms cognizable via the eye.
sn35.97:2.3: When the mind is not stained, joy is born.
sn35.97:2.4: In one who has joy, rapture is born.
sn35.97:2.5: The body of one enraptured at heart grows calm.
sn35.97:2.6: When the body is calm, one feels pleasure.
sn35.97:2.7: Feeling pleasure, the mind becomes centered.
sn35.97:2.8: When the mind is centered, phenomena become manifest.
sn35.97:2.9: When phenomena are manifest, he is reckoned as one who dwells in heedfulness.
sn35.97:2.10: “When a monk dwells with restraint over the tongue…
sn35.97:2.12: “When a monk dwells with restraint over the faculty of the intellect, the mind is not stained with ideas cognizable via the intellect.
sn35.97:2.13: When the mind is not stained, joy is born.
sn35.97:2.14: In one who has joy, rapture is born.
sn35.97:2.15: The body of one enraptured at heart grows calm.
sn35.97:2.16: When the body is calm, one feels pleasure.
sn35.97:2.17: Feeling pleasure, the mind becomes centered.
sn35.97:2.18: When the mind is centered, phenomena become manifest.
sn35.97:2.19: When phenomena are manifest, he is reckoned as one who dwells in heedfulness.
sn35.97:2.20: “This is how one dwells in heedfulness.”
